licensing_board_name: Alaska Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
licensing_notes: Alaska requires contractor registration. General contractors and specialty contractors need a state contractor license. Electrical and mechanical contractors need additional specialty licenses.
insurance_notes: General liability insurance required. Workers comp through private insurance carriers.
payroll_notes: Register for unemployment insurance with Alaska Department of Labor. Can use third-party payroll services.
overview: Alaska requires contractor registration and licensing. No state income tax or sales tax, but local sales taxes may apply. Workers comp through private insurance.

licensing_board_name: Alabama Licensing Board for General Contractors
licensing_notes: General contractors must be licensed for projects over $50,000. Home builders need a separate license from the Home Builders Licensure Board.
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp insurance required. Obtain through private insurers.
payroll_notes: Register with Alabama Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. Can use third-party payroll services like Intuit.
overview: Alabama requires contractor licensing for projects over $50,000. The state has both a General Contractors board and a Home Builders Licensure Board. No sales tax on labor. Workers comp through private insurance.

licensing_notes: Contractors performing work over $20,000 must be licensed. Residential, commercial, and specialty licenses available. Must pass trade exam.
tax_board_name: Arkansas Department of Finance and Administration
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp insurance required through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with AR Division of Workforce Services for unemployment insurance.
overview: Arkansas requires contractor licensing for projects over $20,000. Sales tax applies to materials but not labor for real property improvements. Workers comp through private insurance.

licensing_notes: All contractors must be licensed by the Registrar of Contractors (ROC). Dual license system: residential (R) and commercial (B). Trade exams required. Some trades like painting and flooring still require licensing in AZ.
insurance_notes: General liability insurance required. Workers comp through private insurance.
payroll_notes: Register with AZ Department of Economic Security for unemployment tax. File quarterly.
overview: Arizona has a robust contractor licensing system through the Registrar of Contractors. Both residential and commercial licenses available. Transaction Privilege Tax (TPT) applies to contracting.

licensing_notes: All contractors must be licensed by CSLB for work over $500. Three license classifications: A (General Engineering), B (General Building), C (Specialty). Must pass trade and law exams. Fingerprinting required.
insurance_notes: General liability required. Workers comp available through State Compensation Insurance Fund or private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with EDD for payroll taxes (income tax withholding, unemployment, disability, paid family leave). Can use third-party payroll.
overview: California has one of the most comprehensive contractor licensing systems in the country through the CSLB. All contractors need a license for work over $500. The state has over 40 specialty classifications.

licensing_notes: Colorado does NOT require a state-level contractor license. However, electrical and plumbing contractors need state licenses. Local jurisdictions may require registration or licensing.
insurance_notes: General liability recommended. Workers comp through private carriers required if you have employees.
payroll_notes: Register with CO Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: Colorado is one of the more contractor-friendly states — no state contractor license required except for electrical and plumbing. Check local city/county requirements as many require local registration.

licensing_notes: Home improvement contractors must register with the Department of Consumer Protection. Electrical, plumbing, and HVAC contractors need separate licenses. New home construction contractors need a separate registration.
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with CT Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: Connecticut requires home improvement contractor registration. Trade-specific licenses for electrical, plumbing, and HVAC. No general contractor license at the state level.

licensing_notes: Delaware does NOT require a state contractor license. Some trades (electrical, plumbing, HVAC) may require local licensing. Business licenses may be required by Division of Revenue.
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with Delaware Division of Unemployment Insurance.
overview: Delaware is known for business-friendly incorporation laws. No state contractor license required, but local jurisdictions may have requirements. No sales tax in Delaware.

licensing_board_name: Florida DBPR - Construction Industry Licensing Board
licensing_notes: Florida requires licensing for certified and registered contractors. Certified = statewide, Registered = county-specific. Must pass state exam for certified licenses. GC, building, residential, roofing, sheet metal, plumbing, mechanical, pool, solar, pollutant, underground utility, and specialty licenses available. Some trades like painting and basic flooring do NOT require licensing.
insurance_notes: General liability required. Workers comp through private insurance carriers. FL does NOT have state-controlled workers comp. Exemptions available for sole owners in construction (must apply through DFS).
payroll_notes: Register with FL Department of Revenue for reemployment tax. No state income tax in Florida. No sales tax on labor — only on materials. Can use third-party payroll services.
overview: Florida uses Sunbiz.org for entity formation. You can form an LLC or register a fictitious name (DBA), which is unique to FL. Contractor licensing through DBPR — certified licenses are statewide, registered licenses are county-specific. No sales tax on labor (only materials). No state income tax. Workers comp through private carriers with exemptions available for owners.

licensing_board_name: Georgia Secretary of State - Professional Licensing
licensing_notes: Georgia requires licensing for residential and general contractors. Must pass exam. Electrical, plumbing, and HVAC need separate licenses. Residential Basic Contractor license available for smaller projects.
workers_comp_agency: Georgia State Board of Workers Compensation
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with GA Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: Georgia requires contractor licensing for residential and general contractors. The licensing process involves passing a trade exam. Separate licenses needed for electrical, plumbing, and HVAC trades.

licensing_notes: All contractors must be licensed. General engineering (A), general building (B), and specialty (C) classifications. Must pass exam and show experience.
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp through private carriers. Hawaii has unique TDI (Temporary Disability Insurance) requirement.
payroll_notes: Register with HI Department of Labor for unemployment and TDI. General excise tax applies to all services.
overview: Hawaii requires all contractors to be licensed. Unique general excise tax (GET) applies to all services including construction labor. TDI required for all employers.

licensing_notes: Iowa does NOT require a state general contractor license. Electrical and plumbing contractors need state licenses. Some cities require local registration.
insurance_notes: General liability recommended. Workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with Iowa Workforce Development for unemployment insurance.
overview: Iowa does not require a state general contractor license. Electrical and plumbing trades need state licensing. Check local city/county requirements.

licensing_notes: Public works contractors must register. HVAC, plumbing, and electrical contractors need state licenses. No general contractor license required for private residential work, but some trades require registration.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through State Insurance Fund or private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with Idaho Department of Labor for unemployment. State income tax withholding required.
overview: Idaho has limited state contractor licensing — mainly for public works, electrical, HVAC, and plumbing. Private residential general contracting does not require a state license. Workers comp through State Insurance Fund or private carriers.

licensing_notes: Illinois does NOT require a state general contractor license. Roofing contractors need registration. Electrical and plumbing contractors need state licenses. Local municipalities may require registration.
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with Illinois Department of Employment Security (IDES) for unemployment insurance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: Illinois does not require a state general contractor license, but roofing contractors must register and electrical/plumbing contractors need licenses. Check local city requirements — Chicago has its own licensing system.

licensing_notes: Indiana does NOT require a state general contractor license. Plumbing and electrical contractors need state licenses. Some local jurisdictions require contractor registration.
insurance_notes: General liability recommended. Workers comp through private carriers required with employees.
payroll_notes: Register with Indiana Department of Workforce Development for unemployment insurance.
overview: Indiana is contractor-friendly — no state general contractor license required. Electrical and plumbing need state licensing. Check local requirements.

licensing_notes: Kansas does NOT require a state contractor license. Some cities like Wichita and Kansas City require local licensing. Electrical contractors need municipal licenses.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with Kansas Department of Labor for unemployment insurance.
overview: Kansas does not have a state contractor licensing requirement. Check with local cities for registration requirements.

licensing_notes: Kentucky does NOT require a state general contractor license. HVAC and electrical contractors need state licenses. Local jurisdictions may require contractor registration.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with KY Office of Employment and Training for unemployment insurance.
overview: Kentucky does not require a state general contractor license. HVAC and electrical trades need licensing. Check local requirements.

licensing_board_name: Louisiana State Licensing Board for Contractors
licensing_notes: Contractors performing work over $50,000 must have a state license. Residential and commercial classifications. Must pass exam and show financial responsibility. Subcontractors need licensing for work over $50,000.
insurance_notes: General liability and workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with Louisiana Workforce Commission for unemployment insurance.
overview: Louisiana requires state contractor licensing for work over $50,000. Both residential and commercial classifications available. Must demonstrate financial responsibility.

licensing_notes: Home improvement contractors must register. Construction Supervisor License required for residential work. Electrical, plumbing, and sheet metal need separate licenses.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required.
payroll_notes: Register with MA Department of Unemployment Assistance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: Massachusetts requires Construction Supervisor License for residential work and home improvement contractor registration. Trade licenses for electrical, plumbing, and sheet metal.

sos_name: State Department of Assessments and Taxation
licensing_notes: Home improvement contractors must be licensed by MHIC. Must pass exam and carry insurance. Electrical, plumbing, and HVAC need separate licenses. Commercial work has different requirements.
insurance_notes: Minimum insurance requirements: $50,000 property damage, $100,000 personal injury. Workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with MD Division of Unemployment Insurance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: Maryland requires home improvement contractor licensing through MHIC. Commercial contractors have separate requirements. Must carry minimum insurance levels.

licensing_notes: Maine does NOT require a state general contractor license. Plumbing and electrical contractors need state licenses. Local permits may be required.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with Maine Department of Labor for unemployment insurance.
overview: Maine does not require a state general contractor license. Trade-specific licenses for plumbing and electrical. Relatively simple business formation process.

sos_name: Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs
licensing_notes: Residential builders and maintenance/alteration contractors must be licensed. Must pass exam. Electrical and plumbing need separate licenses.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with Michigan UIA for unemployment insurance.
overview: Michigan requires residential builder licensing. Commercial work may not require state license but check local requirements. Trade licenses for electrical and plumbing.

licensing_board_name: Minnesota Department of Labor and Industry
licensing_notes: Residential contractors must be licensed by DLI. Must pass exam and carry insurance. Commercial contractors need separate licensing in some categories. Electrical and plumbing need separate licenses.
workers_comp_agency: Minnesota Department of Labor and Industry
insurance_notes: Minimum insurance: $100,000 liability required for residential contractor license. Workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with MN Department of Employment and Economic Development for unemployment insurance.
overview: Minnesota requires residential contractor licensing through the Department of Labor and Industry. Must carry minimum liability insurance to get licensed.

licensing_notes: Missouri does NOT require a state general contractor license. Some cities (Kansas City, St. Louis) have local licensing requirements. Electrical contractors may need local licenses.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with MO Division of Employment Security for unemployment insurance.
overview: Missouri does not have a state contractor licensing requirement. Check local city and county requirements, especially in Kansas City and St. Louis.

licensing_notes: Contractors performing work over $50,000 must be licensed. Must pass exam and demonstrate financial responsibility. Residential and commercial classifications.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with Mississippi Department of Employment Security for unemployment insurance.
overview: Mississippi requires contractor licensing for projects over $50,000. Bond required. Both residential and commercial classifications available.

licensing_notes: Montana does NOT require a state general contractor license. Electrical and plumbing need state licenses. Some local jurisdictions may require registration.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through Montana State Fund (state-run). General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with MT Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. No state sales tax.
overview: Montana does not require a state contractor license. Workers comp through the state-run Montana State Fund. No sales tax.

licensing_board_name: NC Licensing Board for General Contractors
licensing_notes: Contractors performing work over $30,000 must be licensed. Must pass exam and demonstrate financial responsibility. Separate licenses for electrical, plumbing, and HVAC.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with NC Division of Employment Security for unemployment insurance.
overview: North Carolina requires contractor licensing for work over $30,000. Must pass exam. Separate trade licenses for electrical, plumbing, fire sprinkler, and HVAC.

licensing_board_name: North Dakota Secretary of State - Contractor Licensing
licensing_notes: Contractors must register with the Secretary of State. Must carry workers comp and general liability insurance. Separate licenses for electrical and plumbing.
workers_comp_agency: North Dakota Workforce Safety and Insurance
tax_board_name: North Dakota Office of State Tax Commissioner
insurance_notes: Workers comp through Workforce Safety and Insurance (state-run monopoly). General liability required for registration.
payroll_notes: Register with Job Service North Dakota for unemployment insurance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: North Dakota requires contractor registration. Workers comp through the state-run Workforce Safety and Insurance (monopoly). No choice of private carriers for workers comp.

licensing_notes: Nebraska does NOT require a state general contractor license. Electrical contractors need a state license. Check local city requirements.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with Nebraska Department of Labor for unemployment insurance.
overview: Nebraska does not have a state contractor licensing requirement. Electrical contractors need state licensing. Check Omaha and Lincoln for local requirements.

licensing_notes: New Hampshire does NOT require a state general contractor license. Electrical and plumbing contractors need state licenses. Local permits required for construction.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with NH Employment Security for unemployment insurance. No state income tax on wages.
overview: New Hampshire does not require a state contractor license. No sales tax and no state income tax on wages. Check local requirements.

licensing_board_name: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
licensing_notes: Home improvement contractors must register with NJ Division of Consumer Affairs. Electrical and plumbing need separate licenses. Must carry insurance to register.
workers_comp_agency: New Jersey Division of Workers Compensation
insurance_notes: Minimum insurance required for HIC registration. Workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with NJ Department of Labor for unemployment and disability insurance. State income tax withholding required.
overview: New Jersey requires home improvement contractor registration. Must carry insurance to register. Separate licenses for electrical and plumbing trades.

licensing_notes: All contractors must be licensed by CID. General building (GB), general engineering (GE), and specialty classifications. Must pass exam.
tax_board_name: New Mexico Taxation and Revenue Department
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required.
payroll_notes: Register with NM Department of Workforce Solutions for unemployment insurance. Gross receipts tax applies to construction services.
overview: New Mexico requires all contractors to be licensed by the Construction Industries Division. Gross receipts tax applies to construction services (similar to sales tax on labor).

licensing_notes: All contractors must be licensed by NSCB. Multiple license classifications. Must pass trade and law exams. Financial statement required. Fingerprinting required.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with NV Employment Security Division for unemployment insurance. No state income tax.
overview: Nevada requires all contractors to be licensed by the State Contractors Board. Must pass exams and provide financial statements. No state income tax.

sos_name: Department of State - Division of Corporations
licensing_notes: New York does NOT have a state contractor license requirement. NYC requires a license from the Department of Buildings for GC, plumbing, electrical, and other trades. Other cities like Buffalo and Rochester have local requirements.
workers_comp_agency: New York State Workers Compensation Board
tax_board_name: New York Department of Taxation and Finance
insurance_notes: Workers comp through NY State Insurance Fund or private carriers. General liability recommended. NYC requires specific insurance levels.
payroll_notes: Register with NY Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. State and local income tax withholding required.
overview: New York does not have a state-level contractor license. NYC has its own robust licensing system through the Department of Buildings. Check local requirements for other cities. Workers comp through State Insurance Fund or private carriers.

licensing_notes: Ohio does NOT require a state general contractor license. HVAC, plumbing, and electrical contractors need state or local licenses. Many cities have local contractor registration requirements.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through Ohio BWC (state-run monopoly). General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with Ohio Department of Job and Family Services for unemployment insurance.
overview: Ohio does not require a state general contractor license. Workers comp through the state-run Bureau of Workers Compensation. Check local city requirements — many Ohio cities have their own licensing.

licensing_notes: Contractors must register with CIB. Electrical, plumbing, and mechanical contractors need state licenses. General contractors need registration for commercial work.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for registration.
payroll_notes: Register with Oklahoma Employment Security Commission for unemployment insurance.
overview: Oklahoma requires contractor registration through the Construction Industries Board. Trade licenses for electrical, plumbing, and mechanical. Bond required for registration.

licensing_notes: All contractors must be licensed by CCB. Must carry insurance, bond, and workers comp. Separate endorsements for commercial and residential. Must pass exam.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through SAIF Corporation (state) or private carriers. General liability and bond required for CCB license.
payroll_notes: Register with Oregon Employment Department for unemployment insurance. No sales tax in Oregon.
overview: Oregon requires all contractors to be licensed by the CCB. Must have insurance, bond, and workers comp. No sales tax in Oregon. Workers comp through SAIF or private carriers.

licensing_notes: Home improvement contractors must register with the Attorney General. PA has a Home Improvement Consumer Protection Act. Electrical and plumbing need local licenses. No state GC license.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers or State Workers Insurance Fund. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with PA Department of Labor for unemployment insurance.
overview: Pennsylvania requires home improvement contractor registration with the Attorney General. No state GC license, but home improvement registration is mandatory. Check local requirements for trade licenses.

licensing_notes: All contractors must register with the Contractors Registration Board. Must carry insurance. Separate licenses for electrical and plumbing.
workers_comp_agency: Rhode Island Division of Workers Compensation
insurance_notes: General liability required for registration. Workers comp through private carriers.
payroll_notes: Register with RI Department of Labor and Training for unemployment insurance.
overview: Rhode Island requires contractor registration through the Contractors Registration Board. Must carry insurance. Sales tax applies to tangible personal property but generally not to construction labor.

licensing_board_name: South Carolina LLR - Contractors Licensing Board
licensing_notes: General and mechanical contractors must be licensed for work over $5,000. Must pass exam and demonstrate financial responsibility. Separate tiers based on project value.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with SC Department of Employment and Workforce for unemployment insurance.
overview: South Carolina requires contractor licensing for work over $5,000. Tiered license system based on maximum project value. Must pass exam.

licensing_notes: South Dakota does NOT require a state contractor license. Electrical and plumbing contractors may need local licenses. Very business-friendly state.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with SD Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. No state income tax.
overview: South Dakota is very business-friendly — no state contractor license, no state income tax. Check local requirements. One of the easiest states to start a construction business.

licensing_notes: Contractors performing work over $25,000 must be licensed. Must pass exam and demonstrate financial responsibility. Separate classifications for general, mechanical, electrical, and specialty contractors.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with TN Department of Labor for unemployment insurance. No state income tax on wages.
overview: Tennessee requires contractor licensing for work over $25,000. No state income tax on wages. Bond required for licensing.

licensing_notes: Texas does NOT require a state general contractor license. Electrical and plumbing contractors need state licenses through TDLR. HVAC needs registration. Many cities (Houston, Dallas, Austin, San Antonio) have local contractor requirements.
workers_comp_agency: Texas Department of Insurance - Division of Workers Compensation
insurance_notes: Workers comp is OPTIONAL in Texas for most employers (unique among states). General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with Texas Workforce Commission for unemployment insurance. No state income tax.
overview: Texas is unique — no state contractor license required and workers comp is optional. No state income tax. Check local city requirements. Electrical and plumbing need TDLR licenses.

licensing_board_name: Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing
licensing_notes: General contractors, specialty contractors, and many trades must be licensed through DOPL. Must pass exam. Separate licenses for electrical, plumbing, and HVAC.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with Utah Department of Workforce Services for unemployment insurance.
overview: Utah requires contractor licensing through DOPL for general and specialty contractors. Must pass trade exam. Relatively straightforward licensing process.

licensing_notes: Contractors must be licensed by DPOR for work over $1,000. Three license tiers: Class A (over $120,000 or 7+ employees), Class B ($10,000-$120,000), Class C ($1,000-$10,000). Must pass exam for Class A and B. Separate certifications for trades.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability required for licensing.
payroll_notes: Register with Virginia Employment Commission for unemployment insurance.
overview: Virginia has a tiered contractor licensing system (Class A, B, C) based on project value and number of employees. Very well-organized system through DPOR.

licensing_notes: Vermont does NOT require a state contractor license. Electrical contractors need a state license. Plumbing contractors need local licensing. Very few state-level requirements for general contracting.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with VT Department of Labor for unemployment insurance.
overview: Vermont does not require a state contractor license for general contracting. One of the simpler states for starting a construction business. Electrical needs state licensing.

licensing_notes: All contractors must be registered with L&I. SOS issues UBI number, then register with DOR for tax accounts. L&I handles contractor registration, workers comp, and can issue citations for operating without a license. Bond required — $12,000 for GC, $6,000 for specialty. Insurance required. Must pass exam for some trades.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through L&I (state-run monopoly). General liability insurance required. L&I manages industrial insurance for all workers.
payroll_notes: Register with Employment Security Department (ESD) for unemployment insurance and worker reporting. Can use Intuit Online Payroll or similar services to handle ESD, L&I, and DOR reporting. B&O tax and retail sales tax apply to construction services including painting and flooring.
overview: Washington has a comprehensive system: SOS (UBI#) → DOR (tax accounts) → L&I (contractor license, workers comp, bond). L&I is a state-run monopoly for workers comp. B&O tax and retail sales tax apply to ALL construction work including painting and flooring. Citations for unlicensed contracting.

licensing_notes: Dwelling contractors and subcontractors must register with DSPS. Must complete an education course. Electrical and plumbing need separate state licenses. Commercial contractors may not need state registration.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with WI Department of Workforce Development for unemployment insurance.
overview: Wisconsin requires dwelling contractor registration with DSPS and completion of an education course. Commercial work has fewer state requirements. Check local city requirements.

licensing_board_name: West Virginia Division of Labor - Contractor Licensing Board
licensing_notes: Contractors performing work over $2,500 must be licensed. Must pass exam and carry insurance. Separate licenses for electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and fire protection.
workers_comp_agency: West Virginia Offices of the Insurance Commissioner
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers (BrickStreet was formerly state-run, now privatized). General liability required.
payroll_notes: Register with WV Division of Employment Security (WorkForce WV) for unemployment insurance.
overview: West Virginia requires contractor licensing for work over $2,500, which is one of the lower thresholds nationwide. Bond required. Workers comp now through private carriers.

licensing_notes: Wyoming does NOT require a state contractor license. Very business-friendly state with minimal regulation. Electrical contractors may need local licensing.
insurance_notes: Workers comp through private carriers. General liability recommended.
payroll_notes: Register with WY Department of Workforce Services for unemployment insurance. No state income tax. No sales tax on services.
overview: Wyoming is one of the most business-friendly states — no state contractor license, no state income tax, no sales tax on services. Minimal regulation. Check local city requirements.
